'''David Howell''' (born {{birth date|1983|11|18|df=y}}) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer for [[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins RL]] in the [[Super League]]. He primarily plays as a {{rlp|ce}}, and can also operate as a {{rlp|wg}}er. Howell previously played for the [[St George Illawarra Dragons]] and the [[Canberra Raiders]] in the [[National Rugby League]].

==NRL career==
Howell made his first grade début for the St George Illawarra Dragons in [[National Rugby League season 2003|2003]], where he made 12 NRL appearances alongside brother [[Michael Howell (rugby league)|Michael Howell]] before joining the [[Canberra Raiders]] in [[National Rugby League season 2005|2005]].<ref name="Quins sign Howell">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/story.php?id=9212|title=Quins sign Howell|publisher=Super League|date=2007-12-04|access-date=2007-12-04}} {{Dead link|date=September 2010|bot=H3llBot}}</ref>

He was released from the final year of his contract with the Canberra Raiders to join the London-based [[London Broncos#Harlequins RL .282006.E2.80.932011.29|Harlequins]].<ref name="Howell to head overseas">{{cite news|url=http://www.news.com.au/dailytelegraph/story/0,22049,22850275-5006066,00.html|title=Howell to head overseas|work=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|date=2007-12-01|access-date=2007-12-01| first=James| last=Phelps}}</ref> The Raiders told Howell he would not have a spot in the team after [[National Rugby League season 2008|2008]] when his contract terminated.<ref name="Loyalty doesn't come into it: Howell">{{cite web|url=http://www.leaguehq.com.au/news/news/loyalty-doesnt-come-into-it-howell/2007/12/04/1196530622026.html|title=Loyalty doesn't come into it: Howell|publisher=LeagueHQ|date=2007-12-04|access-date=2007-12-04|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071206163501/http://www.leaguehq.com.au/news/news/loyalty-doesnt-come-into-it-howell/2007/12/04/1196530622026.html|archive-date=6 December 2007 }}</ref>

Howell scored 15 tries in 48 games for Canberra during the last three seasons.<ref name="English side signs Raiders star">{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,22866766-23214,00.html|title=English side signs Raiders star|publisher=Fox Sports|location=Australia|date=2007-12-04|access-date=2007-12-04}}</ref><ref name="St Helens step up hunt for successor to Daniel Anderson">{{cite news|url=http://www.timesonline.co.uk/tol/sport/rugby/rugby_league/article2994874.ece|archive-url=https://archive.today/20130505135142/http://www.timesonline.co.uk/tol/sport/rugby/rugby_league/article2994874.ece|url-status=dead|archive-date=2013-05-05|title=St Helens step up hunt for successor to Daniel Anderson|work=The Times|date=2007-12-04|access-date=2007-12-04| location=London}}</ref>

==Super League career==
Originally linked with [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]], Howell signed a two-year deal at [[The Stoop]].<ref name="Australian Howell signs for Quins">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/london/7124950.stm|title=Australian Howell signs for Quins|publisher=BBC|date=2007-12-03|access-date=2007-12-03}}</ref><ref name="'Loyalty doesn't come into it' Howell lashes out at Raiders">{{cite web|url=http://canberra.yourguide.com.au/news/sport/rugby-league/loyalty-doesnt-come-into-it-howell-lashes-out-at-raiders/1096839.html|title='Loyalty doesn't come into it' Howell lashes out at Raiders|work=The Canberra Times|date=2007-12-02|access-date=2007-12-02}}{{dead link|date=September 2017 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref>

{{Blockquote|David is a bit of a well kept secret in Australia. I've done a lot of homework on him, including speaking to some of his ex-coaches, and I've been told by them that he's got the potential to be another [[Michael Withers]]...David has got a big frame and I'm expecting him to fill the gap left by [[Paul Sykes (rugby league footballer)|Paul Sykes]]<ref name="'Quins Sign Centre David Howell">{{cite web|url=http://www.quins.co.uk/newspage.ink?nid=38181&newstype=n&matchid=&storytype=RL&Refid=|title=Quins Sign Centre David Howell|publisher=Quins|date=2007-12-03|access-date=2007-12-03}}{{dead link|date=September 2017 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref>|[[Brian McDermott (rugby league)|Brian McDermott]]|''Quins RL Head Coach''}}

He was the sixth addition to the Londoners squad for 2008's [[Super League XIII]].<ref name="Howell Answers Quins Call">{{cite web|url=http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague/07/12/03/RUGBYL_Harlequins.html|title=Howell Answers Quins Call|publisher=Sportinglife|date=2007-12-03|access-date=2007-12-03}}</ref> Howell played in the centres and replaced [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] international [[Paul Sykes (rugby league footballer)|Paul Sykes]].<ref name="Raider Howell joins Harlequins">{{cite web|url=http://www.abc.net.au/news/stories/2007/12/04/2109012.htm|archive-url=https://archive.today/20121231085543/http://www.abc.net.au/news/stories/2007/12/04/2109012.htm|url-status=dead|archive-date=31 December 2012|title=Raider Howell joins Harlequins|publisher=ABC|date=2007-12-04|access-date=2007-12-04}}</ref>

Howell's solid form in the [[Super League]] has sparked rumours of a return to the [[National Rugby League]] (NRL). Whether or not Howell decides to leave [[The Stoop]] is yet to be seen.
